content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Receipt Limits

  - url: https://community.concur.com/t5/Concur-Expense-Forum/Receipt-Limits/m-p/14132

  - source: SAP Concur Community

  - domain: community.concur.com


  ## Content


  # Receipt Limits


  A member of the SAP Concur Community asked what receipt limits other organizations
  use for expenses.


  In the discussion, an SAP Concur Community Manager explained the receipt thresholds
  used for SAP Concur''s own employee expenses. Under SAP Concur''s employee expense
  policy, receipts are required for:


  - cash or out-of-pocket expenses over $25; and

  - corporate card charges over $75.


  The response also explains that other organizations may configure different receipt
  limits. That variation does not change the thresholds stated above for SAP Concur
  employees governed by SAP Concur''s own employee expense policy.


  The payment method is decision-critical. Cash or out-of-pocket spending uses the
  lower $25 threshold, while corporate card charges use the $75 threshold.


  For a SAP Concur employee expense, the receipt requirement is determined by matching
  the expense''s payment method and amount to the applicable threshold. A missing
  receipt does not by itself make every expense unclaimable; the threshold must first
  be applied to the current charge.


  This page provides the governing receipt-threshold rule for SAP Concur employees.
  It does not contain the current employee''s expense amount, payment method, or receipt
  status and therefore cannot decide the current case by itself.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Receipt Limits
url: https://community.concur.com/t5/Concur-Expense-Forum/Receipt-Limits/m-p/14132

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: We Answer: What Are Expense Reports? And Other FAQs.

  - url: https://www.concur.com/blog/article/we-answer-what-are-expense-reports-and-other-faqs

  - source: SAP Concur

  - domain: concur.com


  ## Content


  # We Answer: What Are Expense Reports? And Other FAQs.


  Expense reports are used to manage and track employee business expenses. Common
  examples include travel expenses, client meals or entertainment, mileage, and supplies.


  When employees spend personal funds for business purposes, an expense report provides
  a way to document the expense, attach supporting receipts, and request reimbursement
  from company funds. Expense reports also help organizations monitor spending and
  maintain records for accounting and tax purposes.


  A standard expense report may include the employee name, reimbursement amount, internal
  coding, the amount and date of each expense, a description, and supporting receipts.


  Approval processes vary by organization. A small business may use one approver,
  while a larger organization may require departmental and finance review before reimbursement.


  A clear expense policy helps reduce uncertainty about which expenses are reimbursable.
  Policies may identify reimbursable categories, exclusions, employee responsibilities,
  documentation requirements, and other organization-specific conditions.


  This page explains the general purpose of expense reports. It does not establish
  the receipt threshold for the current expense program and does not decide whether
  a particular missing receipt prevents a specific expense from being claimed.

  '
metadata: {}
title: 'We Answer: What Are Expense Reports? And Other FAQs.'
url: https://www.concur.com/blog/article/we-answer-what-are-expense-reports-and-other-faqs

Your meal charge is **$42.80 USD**, paid with a **corporate card**, under SAP Concur’s U.S. employee expense policy.

The applicable rule is:

- Receipts are required for **corporate-card charges over $75**.
- Receipts are required for **cash or out-of-pocket expenses over $25**.  
  *(Source: [SAP Concur Community – Receipt Limits](https://community.concur.com/t5/Concur-Expense-Forum/Receipt-Limits/m-p/14132))*

Because your charge is **$42.80 on a corporate card**, it is **below the $75 receipt threshold**. The missing receipt should not, by itself, prevent you from claiming it.

You should submit the meal expense with the correct amount, date, business purpose, and attendees or other required details. If the expense system prompts you for a receipt or missing-receipt explanation, follow that prompt; otherwise, no receipt should be required under this threshold rule.

final_answer:
Your meal charge is **$42.80 USD**, paid with a **corporate card**, under SAP Concur’s U.S. employee expense policy.

The applicable rule is:

- Receipts are required for **corporate-card charges over $75**.
- Receipts are required for **cash or out-of-pocket expenses over $25**.  
  *(Source: [SAP Concur Community – Receipt Limits](https://community.concur.com/t5/Concur-Expense-Forum/Receipt-Limits/m-p/14132))*

Because your charge is **$42.80 on a corporate card**, it is **below the $75 receipt threshold**. The missing receipt should not, by itself, prevent you from claiming it.

You should submit the meal expense with the correct amount, date, business purpose, and attendees or other required details. If the expense system prompts you for a receipt or missing-receipt explanation, follow that prompt; otherwise, no receipt should be required under this threshold rule.

text.regex:
(?isx)
^
(?!.*(?:
    (?:this|the|your|current)[\s_-]+(?:expense|charge|meal)
      .{0,70}
      (?:is|falls|sits|remains|was)
      [\s_-]*(?:above|over|greater[\s_-]*than|exceeding)
      .{0,45}
      (?:
          \$?\s*75\b
        | 75[\s_-]*dollar
        | seventy[\s_-]*five
        | (?:receipt[\s_-]*)?(?:threshold|limit|cutoff)
      )
))
(?=.*(?:
    (?:corporate|company|business)[\s_-]*(?:card|credit[\s_-]*card)
  | company[\s_-]*paid[\s_-]*card
))
(?=.*(?:
    (?:below|under|beneath|within|less[\s_-]*than|lower[\s_-]*than)
      .{0,100}
      (?:
          \$?\s*75\b
        | 75[\s_-]*dollar
        | seventy[\s_-]*five
        | (?:applicable[\s_-]*)?(?:receipt[\s_-]*)?(?:threshold|limit|cutoff)
        | (?:it|that)
      )
  | (?:does[\s_-]*not|doesn't|did[\s_-]*not|didn't)
      [\s_-]*(?:reach|exceed|trigger)
      .{0,100}
      (?:
          \$?\s*75\b
        | 75[\s_-]*dollar
        | seventy[\s_-]*five
        | (?:applicable[\s_-]*)?(?:receipt[\s_-]*)?(?:threshold|limit|cutoff)
      )
  | (?:\$?\s*75\b|75[\s_-]*dollar|seventy[\s_-]*five)
      .{0,60}
      (?:receipt[\s_-]*)?(?:threshold|limit|cutoff)
      .{0,80}
      (?:not[\s_-]*(?:reached|triggered|exceeded)|does[\s_-]*not[\s_-]*(?:apply|trigger))
  | receipt(?:s)?
      .{0,180}
      (?:required|necessary|needed)
      .{0,180}
      (?:only[\s_-]+)?(?:over|above|greater[\s_-]*than|exceeding)
      .{0,45}
      (?:\$?\s*75\b|75[\s_-]*dollar|seventy[\s_-]*five)
  | (?:corporate|company|business)[\s_-]*(?:card|credit[\s_-]*card)
      .{0,100}
      (?:charges?|transactions?|expenses?)?
      .{0,50}
      (?:over|above|greater[\s_-]*than|exceeding)
      .{0,45}
      (?:\$?\s*75\b|75[\s_-]*dollar|seventy[\s_-]*five)
  | (?:corporate|company|business)[\s_-]*(?:card|credit[\s_-]*card)
      .{0,100}
      (?:receipt[\s_-]*)?(?:threshold|limit|cutoff)
      .{0,80}
      (?:over|above|greater[\s_-]*than|exceeding)
      .{0,45}
      (?:\$?\s*75\b|75[\s_-]*dollar|seventy[\s_-]*five)
))
(?=.*(?:
    receipt(?:s)?[\s_-]+(?:is|are|was|were)?[\s_-]*not[\s_-]+
      (?:required|necessary|needed)
  | no[\s_-]+receipt[\s_-]+(?:is[\s_-]+)?(?:required|necessary|needed)
  | (?:this|the|your|current|it)[\s_-]+(?:expense|charge|meal|one)?
      .{0,45}
      (?:does[\s_-]*not|doesn't|will[\s_-]*not|won't)
      [\s_-]*(?:require|need)
      .{0,30}(?:a[\s_-]+)?receipt
  | (?:missing|no)[\s_-]+receipt
      .{0,180}
      (?:
          does[\s_-]*not
        | doesn't
        | will[\s_-]*not
        | won't
        | not[\s_-]*by[\s_-]*itself
      )
      .{0,120}
      (?:block|prevent|bar|disqualify|make.{0,25}(?:ineligible|unclaimable))
  | (?:
        (?:you|the[\s_-]*employee)[\s_-]+
          (?:can|may|could|are[\s_-]+allowed[\s_-]+to|are[\s_-]+eligible[\s_-]+to)
      | (?:you|the[\s_-]*employee)[\s_-]+should[\s_-]+be[\s_-]+able[\s_-]+to
    )
      [\s_-]*(?:still[\s_-]+)?(?:proceed[\s_-]+to[\s_-]+)?
      (?:claim|submit)
  | (?:this|the|your|current)[\s_-]+(?:expense|charge|meal)
      .{0,60}
      (?:
          (?:can|may|could)[\s_-]+be[\s_-]+(?:claimed|submitted)
        | (?:is|remains)[\s_-]+(?:eligible|allowed|claimable)
      )
  | (?:can|may|could|should[\s_-]+be[\s_-]+able[\s_-]+to)
      .{0,50}
      (?:claim|submit)
      .{0,110}
      (?:without|despite|even[\s_-]*without)
      .{0,45}
      (?:a[\s_-]+)?(?:missing[\s_-]+)?receipt
))
.*
$
